spec_match.rationale: Explicitly demonstrates the spec carve-out: top-level navigations stay origin-partitioned while subframe loads are storage-key-partitioned. That is exactly the v137 wording.

interactive.rationale: Multiple buttons drive same-origin and sandboxed iframe loads of a blob URL, with success/failure surfacing live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on feature index nails the page content.

edge_cases.rationale: Missing the third arm of the spec — top-level navigation TO the blob URL from a different top-level site. Without that, the carve-out story is incomplete.

openQuestions.title: Add the top-level navigation carve-out demo
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows same-origin iframe (works) and sandboxed cross-site iframe (blocked). It does not show the carve-out where window.open(blobUrl) from a different top-level site still navigates. Add a `Open in new tab` button that exercises this.

openQuestions.title: Show the full storage key triple
openQuestions.detail: Surface (top-level site, origin, has-cross-site-ancestor) per attempt so the user can read off the partitioning rule.

summary: Strongest concept in the feature for the spec's wording — explicit on the navigation vs fetching distinction. Add the top-level navigation case to round it out.
